summary: For managing vitamin D toxicity, priority should lie not just theoretically or conceptually correct approaches such as those involving indirect interventions via altering metabolic pathways through use of ketoconazole; instead, practical efficacy guides treatment choices towards measures proven effective including cessation of excessive vitamin D supplementation alongside supportive care strategies aimed specifically addressing hypercalcemia when present—such support often includes administration of bisphophonates where necessary—and occasionally corticosteroids may also play a role depending upon severity symptoms exhibited by individual patient cases presenting acutely requiring urgent intervention due to life-threatening manifestations necessitating aggressive management protocols designed precisely tackle acute crises promptly effectively preventing further harm ensuring optimal recovery outcomes achievable only thorough multidisciplinary approach combining pharmacological therapies symptom control monitoring follow-up adjustments based latest available scientific knowledge guiding decision-making processes throughout entire course illness progression attainable goals set pursued relentlessly until complete remission achieved sustained stable condition maintained indefinitely barring unforeseen adverse events complicating prognosis negatively
